summary.intent: Implement GitHub issue #300 — add a ColorPicker agnostic-core state machine to the ars-ui Rust component library, following the conventions established by the 7 sibling color components (#297–#299).
summary.outcome: Fully implemented the ColorPicker machine (State/Event/Effect/Context/Props/Messages/Machine/Part/Api) with 79 unit tests, 26 insta snapshot fixtures, spec-conformance test, proptest module, and spec sync. All CI gates passed (4541 lib tests, clippy clean, 98.5% line / 90.1% branch coverage). Committed and PR opened to main closing #300.

repo: The spec's §1.7 machine section was written in a prototype style using non-existent helpers (add_click_outside_listener_multi, is_eyedropper_available, with_named_effect) and string-named effects — it cannot be transcribed literally; canonical patterns come from the sibling color components.

ComponentPart requires Eq + Hash on all Part enum variants; ColorValue contains f64 and cannot satisfy this, so Part::Swatch must carry only an index and resolve color from a swatches prop (mirroring color_swatch_picker's colors prop).
ColorChannel was missing Hash, which blocked its use in Part::ChannelSlider{channel} — required an additive derive in ars-core.
MessageFn uses explicit per-signature From impls; &ColorValue closures needed two new impls added to ars-core/src/message_fn.rs alongside existing ColorChannel/ColorNameParts impls.
Positioning types live in crate::overlay::positioning (ars-components' own DOM-free copy), not ars_dom — using ..Default::default() covers unspecified fields.
The highlight snapshot suite is gated behind the i18n feature; running insta --unreferenced=reject without --features i18n produces false orphan failures.
The native coverage gate enforces 90% branch coverage on ars-components; branch coverage is significantly harder to hit than line coverage (98.5% line vs 90.0% branch initially).
matches!(expr, Pattern if guard) in tests creates a synthetic uncovered branch (the false/panic arm) that inflates the branch denominator — prefer assert_eq! when the enum derives PartialEq.
The spec snippet-compile gate concatenates per-file rust code blocks with a preamble; plain ```rust fences are compiled, so new blocks must not introduce unresolvable identifiers.
cargo xtask spec validate covers 340 spec files and is separate from the snippet-compile gate — both must pass.

code.finding: context_relevant_props_changed uses || chaining across 8 props; each operand needs a test changing only that prop in isolation to cover the true branch, since short-circuit evaluation means later operands are never evaluated when an earlier one is true.
Defensive div-by-zero guard — unreachable for all real ColorChannels (all have nonzero range); intentionally left uncovered per post-implementation-audit convention.
RTL ArrowLeft branches in the color area and channel slider keydown handlers require an explicit dir: Dir::Rtl prop in tests to exercise; initially missed in coverage.
ColorChannel needed #[derive(Hash)] added — previously only derived Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord.
ars-components carries its own slimmer PositioningOptions/Offset types here (not re-exported from ars_dom); ..Default::default() covers all optional fields.

workflow: Run clippy -D warnings before tests during implementation — it surfaces convention findings (const fn eligibility, doc backtick formatting, #[expect] on intentional pattern arms) that are faster to fix before the full test suite.
Use llvm-cov uncovered-line output first, then parse the lcov branch data separately to find uncovered branch arms — they are different failure modes requiring different fixes.
Replacing matches!(…) guards with assert_eq! in tests removes synthetic branch denominator inflation without changing semantic coverage.

The post-implementation audit follows a fixed 3-phase structure: (1) spec/impl drift, (2) convention/lint (multiple rounds until consecutive clean), (3) coverage — completing all three before committing catches issues that tests alone miss.
Splicing large spec sections (>100 lines) by line range via a shell heredoc is more reliable than an Edit old_string match when the block contains many special characters.
cargo xci --profile fast is the mandatory pre-push gate; run it as a background task and await the completion notification rather than polling.

friction: Spec §1.7 machine section was written in a non-canonical prototype style — discovering the mismatch required reading sibling implementations and dispatching agents to map real effect/platform patterns before writing any code.
ColorValue's f64 fields made Part::Swatch{color} impossible (Eq+Hash unsatisfiable), requiring a design change (swatches prop + index-only Part variant) that was not anticipated in the spec.

Compile errors on first build: wrong positioning import path (ars_dom vs crate::overlay::positioning), missing ColorChannel Hash, missing &ColorValue MessageFn From impls — all required cross-crate edits before the module could link.
Coverage gate failed at exactly the 90.0% boundary (was ~89.97% branch) after initial test pass — required a second targeted round of branch-coverage additions and a rerun of the ~10-minute gate.
matches! guard patterns in tests created synthetic uncovered branches that inflated the denominator, masking the true gap — required converting them to assert_eq! to get an accurate signal.
The i18n feature gate caused insta --unreferenced=reject to report false orphan failures for highlight snapshots, requiring a re-run with --features i18n to get a clean result.
Pre-existing broken rustdoc links in table/data-grid components (123 crate-wide warnings) surfaced under -D warnings during doc build, adding noise to the verification step.
open_items: 123 pre-existing broken rustdoc intra-doc links in table/data-grid components — out of scope for this PR but should be tracked as tech debt.
Line 1370 defensive div-by-zero guard is intentionally uncovered; if a zero-range ColorChannel variant is ever added, this line will need a test.
Codex-review loop was mentioned as a post-PR step but is not shown as completed in the transcript — may still be pending.
